name: Software development service with 23 VAT should be OK after category is implemented

metadata.rationale: Clear match to software_it_services_23. Invoice VAT 23% matches expected rate. Baseline happy path — correct category, correct rate, no GTU code supplied.

input.supplierName: Example Software House Sp. z o.o.

name: Bare Chopin from Empik — model resolves to books (5%), invoice at 23% is Critical
knownLimitation: qwen3-embedding:0.6b places 'Chopin' (ambiguous: composer vs vodka brand) closer to software_23 than books_5 in its embedding space, because books_5 has music-related negative examples (CD, concert) that score high for the word 'Chopin'. The supplier channel (Empik = bookstore) currently only affects candidate ranking, not the classification threshold. Fixing this requires either a larger embedding model or direct supplier-to-category boosting.

metadata.rationale: qwen3-embedding consistently maps 'Chopin' from Empik (bookstore) to books_5 (5% VAT). The model correctly identifies the most likely product and flags the invoice VAT rate 23% as a Critical mismatch. Ambiguity cannot be expressed without a second competing category clearing the ambiguity floor.

name: Alcohol item with 8 VAT should be critical mismatch

metadata.rationale: Wódka Chopin 0,7l from an alcohol wholesaler is unambiguously spirits (23%). Charging 8% is a critical VAT mismatch. This case must never be rated Ok or Warning.

name: GTU_01 alcohol with 8% VAT — GTU fast-path resolves category, VAT mismatch is Critical

metadata.rationale: GTU_01 unambiguously identifies alcoholic beverages (always 23%). The fast-path should resolve the category without embedding. Charging 8% is Critical. This case verifies the GTU bypass and correct severity mapping.

name: Construction subcontract without reverse charge applied — structural Critical

metadata.rationale: GTU_10 construction subcontracts require reverse charge (P_18=true). Charging VAT 23% with reverseChargeApplied=false is a structural violation detectable before embedding. Must never be rated Ok or Warning.

name: GTU_12 (software) with BHP training description — GTU trusted, VAT matches, result Ok

metadata.rationale: GTU_12 explicitly classifies this as software/licences (23%). The description 'Szkolenie BHP' is suspicious (BHP training might be exempt) but in v1 the GTU code takes precedence over the description. Invoice VAT 23% matches GTU_12 category. Result is Ok — GTU is the authoritative signal.

name: Construction service without GTU — ambiguous residential vs commercial rate variant

metadata.rationale: Usługi budowlane without a GTU code matches construction_services which has rate variants (8% residential, 23% commercial). Without GTU_10 to confirm, the engine cannot determine which rate applies. Category has HasRateVariants=true, so result is Ambiguous with different possible rates.

name: Pharmaceutical product with English description — embedding handles bilingual names at correct 8% rate

metadata.rationale: English product description 'Ibuprofen 200mg tablets' should embed similarly to the Polish positive examples in pharmaceuticals_8. Tests that qwen3-embedding handles English pharmaceutical names without GTU assistance. No GTU provided to force the embedding path.

name: Generic consulting description — ambiguous between IT and legal consulting, both 23%, should be Warning

metadata.rationale: Doradztwo i konsultacje biznesowe is genuinely ambiguous between software_it_services_23 (IT consulting) and consulting_legal_23 (management/legal consulting). Both expected rates are 23% and the invoice rate is 23% — all candidates are consistent. Result should be Warning (not Alert), vatValidationStatus Match. This verifies the CategoryAmbiguousButVatConsistent branch.

name: Technical industrial gas — no category match in seed data, should be Alert

metadata.rationale: Argon industrial gas does not match any defined category (IT services, alcohol, books, construction, pharmaceuticals, medical, food, electronics, consulting). Tests the NotMatched path. This case is a placeholder until an industrial gases / chemicals category is added.

name: Vodka from a bookstore — supplier context must not override confident alcohol classification

metadata.rationale: Wódka Chopin 0,7l is unambiguously alcohol (23%) even when sold by Empik (a bookstore chain). Distinguishes from case-002 which uses the bare 'Chopin' brand — here the full product name is decisive. Tests that supplier-item mismatch does not downgrade a confident item classification.

name: Medical consultation charged at standard 23% — VAT-exempt service misclassified

metadata.rationale: Porada lekarska from a licensed clinic is VAT-exempt (ZW, rate 0 in model). Charging 23% is a critical mismatch. This case must never be rated Ok or Warning — it represents a common and costly classification error where healthcare services are incorrectly treated as taxable.

name: Electronics with GTU_06 and split payment correctly flagged — standard rate should be Ok

metadata.rationale: Laptop with GTU_06 (electronics), VAT 23% correct, splitPaymentRequired=true correctly annotated. Tests that a properly structured electronics invoice returns Ok. SplitPaymentRequired is accepted-but-not-acted-on in v1 engine; this case verifies it does not affect severity when rate is correct.

name: Mineral water with erroneous GTU_01 — engine trusts GTU code and finds VAT mismatch

metadata.rationale: Mineral water is an 8% food product but GTU_01 (alcoholic beverages) is erroneously present on the invoice. V1 engine trusts GTU_01, resolves category as alcohol_spirits_23 (expected 23%), finds invoice rate 8% — Critical. Demonstrates that incorrect GTU codes on the source invoice cause incorrect engine results; input data quality is critical.

input.supplierName: Hurtownia Napojów Beta Sp. z o.o.

name: SaaS subscription with reverse charge applied but positive VAT rate — structural violation

metadata.rationale: reverseChargeApplied=true means the buyer self-assesses VAT; the invoice must show 0 VAT. Charging 23% with P_18=true is a structural contradiction caught by StructuralCheckStep before GTU resolution. GTU_12 is present but irrelevant — the structural check fires first.

name: Wholegrain bread at correct 5% — basic food happy path

metadata.rationale: Chleb razowy is basic bread — Annex 10 food product at 5%. Invoice rate matches. Tests the food_basic_5 category happy path without embedding ambiguity.

name: UHT milk charged at standard 23% — basic food misclassified at full rate

metadata.rationale: Mleko UHT is a basic dairy product at 5% (Annex 10). Charging 23% is a critical VAT mismatch. This case must never be rated Ok or Warning.

input.supplierName: Hurtownia Spożywcza Mleczarnia Sp. z o.o.

name: Red wine charged at 8% food rate — alcohol is always 23%, never qualifies for food rates

metadata.rationale: Wine is an alcoholic beverage (23%) regardless of its food-like appearance. Charging 8% is a common classification error — wine classified as a food product. This case must never be rated Ok or Warning. Tests the alcohol_spirits_23 category coverage of wine.

input.supplierName: Hurtownia Win i Alkoholi Południe Sp. z o.o.

name: Ground coffee at correct 8% — processed food happy path
knownLimitation: qwen3-embedding:0.6b places coffee in a dense beverage cluster where food_beverages_8 (adj=0.265) and food_basic_5 (adj=0.237) are only 0.028 apart — below the 0.04 margin threshold needed for a confident Matched result. Adding coffee as a food_basic negative suppresses the ambiguity for coffee but also penalises milk queries (coffee-to-milk similarity ≈ 0.61) which breaks case-017. A larger embedding model or a keyword fast-path for common beverage names would resolve this.

metadata.rationale: Kawa mielona is a processed/packaged food product (Annex 3, 8%). Invoice rate matches. Tests the food_beverages_8 category happy path.

input.supplierName: Hurtownia Spożywcza Delta Sp. z o.o.

name: Restaurant meal charged at 23% — catering food misclassified at full rate

name: Dietary supplement — borderline between basic food (5%) and pharmaceutical (8%)

metadata.rationale: Suplement diety witamina D is a genuine borderline: dietary supplements may be classified as basic food (5%, Annex 10) or as health products (8%, Annex 3) depending on their composition and marketing. The engine cannot resolve this from the description alone. Different candidate rates → Alert.
